steps.description: Review and align shared auth/session constants and error-code usage, confirming the shared package exports the required session timeout and HTTP/error constants used by the API auth stack.

Update the audit logging helper so all authentication, session, workspace-authorization, and API-token events required by the new auth flow can be consistently written to audit_logs without changing request behavior on audit failures.

Rework the auth middleware implementation to fully support Bearer-token auth first, session-cookie fallback with 15-minute inactivity and 12-hour absolute timeouts, workspace membership validation, sliding cookie refresh, and Express Request augmentation for user/session context, plus super-admin/workspace-admin/workspace-access middleware behavior and uniform error response shape.

Refactor the auth routes to implement the requested login/logout/me/extend-session/session behavior using bcrypt password verification, case-insensitive email lookup, secure random session creation, cookie handling, audit logging, and consistent success/error payloads.

Refine the API token CRUD routes for CLI use so they rely on the new auth middleware contract, preserve token hashing and lifecycle rules, emit required audit events, and ensure all error responses match the mandated `{ success: false, error: { code, message } }` shape.

Fix and complete application-level security wiring in the Express app: helmet+CSP, credentialed CORS, cookie-parser, express-session, CSRF token endpoint before CSRF protection, conditional csrf-sync bypass for Bearer tokens, login/API rate limiters with the required exemptions, and correct middleware application across mounted routes.

Update unit tests for the auth middleware to cover the finalized Bearer-token precedence, session timeout behavior, workspace membership enforcement, sliding cookie refresh, and standardized error codes/messages expected from the new middleware implementation.

Update the auth route integration tests to validate the complete session-cookie auth flow, CSRF token acquisition, login cookie issuance, /api/auth/me session reuse, session extension/session introspection, logout invalidation, and expired-session 401 SESSION_EXPIRED responses against the updated app wiring.

Adjust the API-token route tests to match any response-shape and audit/auth-contract changes introduced by the full authentication system implementation while continuing to validate deterministic SHA-256 hashing behavior.

messages.content:
Implement the full authentication and session management system for Ship's Express API. This covers session-cookie auth, API token auth, CSRF protection, and rate limiting.

Create `middleware/auth.ts` with an `authMiddleware` that: (1) checks for Bearer token first (API token auth via SHA-256 hash lookup in api_tokens table, validates not revoked/expired, updates last_used_at), (2) falls back to session cookie auth (reads `session_id` from cookies, validates session exists, checks 15-minute inactivity timeout and 12-hour absolute timeout per NIST SP 800-63B-4 AAL2, verifies workspace membership, updates last_activity, refreshes cookie with sliding expiration). Attach userId, workspaceId, isSuperAdmin, isApiToken to the Express Request. Also create `superAdminMiddleware`, `workspaceAdminMiddleware`, and `workspaceAccessMiddleware`.

Create `routes/auth.ts` with: POST /api/auth/login (email+password, bcrypt.compare, case-insensitive email lookup, creates cryptographically secure session via crypto.randomBytes(32), sets httpOnly/secure/sameSite=strict cookie, returns user+workspace data), POST /api/auth/logout (deletes session, clears cookie), GET /api/auth/me (returns current user+workspaces), POST /api/auth/extend-session, GET /api/auth/session. Create `routes/api-tokens.ts` for CLI token CRUD.
